questions.question: What is the relationship between a class and an object in Java?
options: A class is a copy of an object
A class is a blueprint and an object is an instance of that blueprint
An object is a blueprint and a class is an instance of that blueprint
A class and an object are the same thing
questions.answer: A class is a blueprint and an object is an instance of that blueprint

questions.question: Which keyword is used to create a new object from a class in Java?
options: create
instance
new
object
questions.answer: new

questions.question: Where are instance variables declared in a Java class?
options: Inside a method
Inside a constructor only
Inside the class but outside any method
Outside the class definition
questions.answer: Inside the class but outside any method

questions.question: What is the default value of an instance variable of type int?
options: The variable has no default and must be initialized
null

questions.question: What is the default value of an instance variable of type String?
options: An empty string ""
null
"null"
The variable has no default and must be initialized
questions.answer: null

questions.question: What happens if you try to use a local variable before initializing it?
options: It uses the default value of 0
It uses null as the default value
A compilation error occurs
A runtime exception is thrown
questions.answer: A compilation error occurs

questions.question: What does the private access modifier do when applied to a field?
options: It allows the field to be accessed from any class in the same package
It restricts access to the field to within the class only
It makes the field read-only from all locations
It prevents the field from being assigned a value
questions.answer: It restricts access to the field to within the class only

questions.question: Which OOP concept is most closely related to using private fields with public methods?
options: Inheritance
Polymorphism
Encapsulation
Abstraction
questions.answer: Encapsulation

questions.question: What distinguishes a static variable from an instance variable?
options: A static variable is declared inside a method
A static variable belongs to the class and is shared by all objects
A static variable must be initialized before use like a local variable
A static variable cannot be accessed using the class name
questions.answer: A static variable belongs to the class and is shared by all objects

options: A compilation error occurs

questions.question: What is variable shadowing in Java?
options: When a variable is declared but never used
When a local variable has the same name as an instance variable, hiding it
When two classes have fields with the same name
When a variable is declared as private and cannot be seen
questions.answer: When a local variable has the same name as an instance variable, hiding it

questions.question: How do you access the instance variable when it is shadowed by a local variable of the same name?
options: Use the super keyword
Use the this keyword
Use the class name as a prefix
You cannot access the instance variable when it is shadowed
questions.answer: Use the this keyword

questions.question: What is the default value of an instance variable of type boolean?
options: true
false
null
It has no default and must be initialized
questions.answer: false

questions.question: Which of the following correctly declares a private field in Java?
options: private: double balance;
double private balance;
private double balance;
double balance private;
questions.answer: private double balance;

questions.question: What is the lifetime of a local variable?
options: It lives as long as the object exists
It lives as long as the program runs
It lives only while the method in which it is declared is executing
It lives until the garbage collector removes it
questions.answer: It lives only while the method in which it is declared is executing

options: The value is set to 1000 successfully
A runtime exception is thrown
A compilation error occurs
The value is set to the default value instead
questions.answer: A compilation error occurs

questions.question: What is a getter method?
options: A method that creates a new object
A method that returns the value of a private field
A method that sets the value of a private field
A method that deletes a field from the object
questions.answer: A method that returns the value of a private field

questions.question: What is a key benefit of using setter methods instead of allowing direct access to fields?
options: Setter methods make the program run faster
Setter methods allow you to validate input before assigning values
Setter methods automatically encrypt the data
Setter methods are required by the Java compiler
questions.answer: Setter methods allow you to validate input before assigning values

questions.question: How should a static variable typically be accessed?
options: Through an object reference only
Using the class name, such as Counter.totalCounters
Using the this keyword
Static variables cannot be accessed outside their class
questions.answer: Using the class name, such as Counter.totalCounters

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the scope of an instance variable?
options: It is accessible only within the method where it is declared
It is accessible throughout the entire class
It is accessible only within the constructor
It is accessible from any class without restriction
questions.answer: It is accessible throughout the entire class

questions.question: Given a Student class with a static field 'private static int nextId = 1000', what is the purpose of making nextId static?
options: To ensure each Student object gets its own separate copy of nextId
To ensure all Student objects share one counter so each student gets a unique ID
To prevent nextId from being modified after initialization
To allow nextId to be used as a local variable inside methods
questions.answer: To ensure all Student objects share one counter so each student gets a unique ID

questions.question: What is the lifetime of a static (class) variable?
options: It lives only while the method it is declared in is executing
It lives as long as the object that created it
It lives as long as the program is running
It lives until the garbage collector removes it
questions.answer: It lives as long as the program is running

questions.question: What happens in memory when you execute: Person p = new Person();?
options: A new Person object is created on the stack and p is a copy of it
A new Person object is created on the heap and p holds a reference to it
The Person class is duplicated in memory
A new Person object replaces the Person class in memory
questions.answer: A new Person object is created on the heap and p holds a reference to it

questions.question: Which of the following statements about instance variables and local variables is correct?
options: Both instance variables and local variables receive default values
Neither instance variables nor local variables receive default values
Instance variables receive default values but local variables do not
Local variables receive default values but instance variables do not
questions.answer: Instance variables receive default values but local variables do not

questions.question: Why is it considered good practice to make fields private and provide public getter and setter methods?
options: It allows external code to bypass validation logic when needed
It protects the internal state of the object and allows controlled access with validation
It makes the code shorter and easier to write
It is required by the Java language specification for all classes
questions.answer: It protects the internal state of the object and allows controlled access with validation
